# Source Index

Converted from `03_source_index.csv` so the pack remains Markdown-only.

| key | category | priority | title | authors | year | type | access | url | bias | why |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| vakratsas_ambler_1999 | Advertising effectiveness | Essential | How Advertising Works: What Do We Really Know? | Demetrios Vakratsas; Tim Ambler | 1999 | Peer-reviewed review paper | Publisher/JSTOR page; library access may be needed | https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/002224299906300103 | Academic review; older but foundational; not platform-funded | Breaks the simplistic ad -> belief -> purchase model; maps cognition, affect, and experience effects. |
| sethuraman_tellis_briesch_2011 | Advertising effectiveness | Essential | How Well Does Advertising Work? Generalizations from Meta-Analysis of Brand Advertising Elasticities | Raj Sethuraman; Gerard J. Tellis; Richard A. Briesch | 2011 | Peer-reviewed meta-analysis | Publisher page; author copies/mirrors may exist | https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1509/jmkr.48.3.457 | Academic meta-analysis; publication and method heterogeneity still matter | Gives empirical benchmarks for how much advertising tends to move sales; prevents magical thinking. |
| korkames_stanley_stremersch_2025 | Advertising effectiveness | Essential | Meta-analysis of Advertising Effectiveness: New Insights from Improved Bias Corrections | Joseph Korkames; T. D. Stanley; Stefan Stremersch | 2025/2026 | Peer-reviewed meta-analysis / bias correction | Open PDF from author university page | https://personal.eur.nl/stremersch/artikels/Korkames_Stanley_and_Stremersch_IJRM_2025_print.pdf | Academic; explicitly focused on correcting publication/misspecification/aggregation bias | Important antidote to overconfident marketing claims; focuses on how bias inflates measured ad effects. |
| rosengren_eisend_koslow_dahlen_2020 | Advertising effectiveness | High | A Meta-Analysis of When and How Advertising Creativity Works | Sara Rosengren; Martin Eisend; Scott Koslow; Micael Dahlen | 2020 | Peer-reviewed meta-analysis | Publisher page and open author PDFs available | https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/0022242920929288 | Academic; creativity effects depend heavily on measurement and context | Teaches when creative advertising changes responses and when it does not. |
| binet_field_long_short | Advertising effectiveness | High | The Long and the Short of It: Balancing Short and Long-Term Marketing Strategies | Les Binet; Peter Field | 2013 | Practitioner/industry effectiveness analysis | IPA report; versions/slides publicly available | https://ipa.co.uk/knowledge/publications-reports/the-long-and-the-short-of-it-balancing-short-and-long-term-marketing-strategies | Practitioner and award-case database; useful but not neutral academic evidence | Separates short-term activation from long-term brand building; useful for avoiding click-obsession. |
| winer_2012_advertising_work | Advertising effectiveness | Useful | Does Advertising Work? Evidence from the Past and Some New Evidence | Russ Winer | 2012 | Academic presentation / review | Open PDF | https://www.gc.cuny.edu/sites/default/files/2021-07/Does-Avertising-Work--Winer.pdf | Lecture deck; interpret as synthesis, not primary evidence | Readable overview of advertising elasticities and historical evidence. |
| sharp_how_brands_grow | Brand strategy | High | How Brands Grow | Byron Sharp | 2010 | Book / evidence-based marketing | Buy/borrow; summaries online | https://marketingscience.info/news-and-insights/grow-brand-grow | Ehrenberg-Bass school has strong viewpoint; compare with segmentation/positioning literature | Mental availability, physical availability, distinctive assets; useful counterweight to narrow performance marketing. |
| distinctive_assets_guide | Brand strategy | Useful | The DNA of Distinctive Brands: Guide to Distinctive Brand Assets | Distinctive BAT | 2024 | Practitioner guide | Open PDF | https://www.distinctivebat.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/The-DNA-of-Distinctive-Brands-Distinctive-BAT-Guide-To-DBAs.pdf | Commercial consultancy; useful framework but verify with independent evidence | Practical way to apply distinctive brand asset thinking to digital products. |
| goldfarb_tucker_online_advertising | Digital advertising measurement | Essential | Online Advertising | Avi Goldfarb; Catherine Tucker | 2011/2014 | Academic handbook chapter | Open author PDF | https://www-2.rotman.utoronto.ca/~agoldfarb/OnlineAdvertising.pdf | Academic overview; older but still foundational | Explains targeting, measurement, privacy, and economic differences between online and offline advertising. |
| goldfarb_tucker_privacy_advertising | Digital advertising measurement | High | Privacy Regulation and Online Advertising | Avi Goldfarb; Catherine Tucker | 2011 | Peer-reviewed research paper | Open PDF | https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/Privacy-Regulation-and-Online-Advertising.pdf | Academic; context predates today’s privacy stack but mechanisms matter | Shows how privacy rules and targeting interact with ad effectiveness. |
| lewis_rao_reiley_digital_frontier | Digital advertising measurement | Essential | Measuring the Effects of Advertising: The Digital Frontier | Randall A. Lewis; Justin M. Rao; David H. Reiley | 2013/2015 | NBER/academic chapter | Open NBER PDF | https://www.nber.org/system/files/working_papers/w19520/w19520.pdf | Authors have worked at ad platforms; still one of the strongest measurement papers | Clicks are easy to count; causal ad lift is hard. This paper teaches that distinction. |
| lewis_rao_unfavorable_economics | Digital advertising measurement | Essential | The Unfavorable Economics of Measuring the Returns to Advertising | Randall A. Lewis; Justin M. Rao | 2015 | Peer-reviewed field experiment evidence | Publisher/SSRN; open copies available | https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=2367103 | Strong empirical design; not a “marketing is useless” paper, a measurement precision paper | Shows why measured ROI confidence intervals can be huge; prevents dashboard worship. |
| gordon_zettelmeyer_bhargava_chapsky_2018 | Digital advertising measurement | Essential | A Comparison of Approaches to Advertising Measurement: Evidence from Big Field Experiments at Facebook | Brett R. Gordon; Florian Zettelmeyer; Neha Bhargava; Daniel Chapsky | 2018/2019 | Peer-reviewed field experiment comparison | Open author PDF | https://www.kellogg.northwestern.edu/faculty/gordon_b/files/fb_comparison.pdf | Facebook experiment data; platform context matters, but methodology lessons are broad | Contrasts randomized experiments with observational measurement and shows why endogeneity matters. |
| johnson_lewis_nubbemeyer_2017 | Digital advertising measurement | High | The Online Display Ad Effectiveness Funnel & Carryover: Lessons from 432 Field Experiments | Garrett A. Johnson; Randall A. Lewis; Elmar I. Nubbemeyer | 2017 | Field experiment meta-study | Open PDF | https://marketing.wharton.upenn.edu/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/Johnson-Garrett-PAPER-VERSION-2.pdf | Display-ad platform setting; still valuable scale and causal design | Useful for understanding small ad effects, funnel stages, and carryover. |
| blake_nosko_tadelis_2015 | Digital advertising measurement | High | Consumer Heterogeneity and Paid Search Effectiveness: A Large-Scale Field Experiment | Thomas Blake; Chris Nosko; Steven Tadelis | 2015 | Peer-reviewed field experiment | Open author PDF | https://faculty.haas.berkeley.edu/stadelis/BNT_ECMA_rev.pdf | eBay context; generalize carefully | Shows paid search can be weak for already-known brands but useful for new/infrequent users. |
| online_ads_offline_sales | Digital advertising measurement | Useful | Online Ads and Offline Sales: Measuring the Effects of Retail Advertising via a Controlled Experiment on Yahoo! | Randall A. Lewis; David H. Reiley | 2014 | Peer-reviewed controlled experiment | Open author PDF | https://www.davidreiley.com/papers/OnlineAdsOfflineSales.pdf | Retail context; still useful for click vs non-click impact | Important reminder that many ad effects may happen without clicks. |
| choi_mela_balseiro_leary_2019 | Digital advertising measurement | Useful | Online Display Advertising Markets: A Literature Review and Future Directions | Hana Choi; Carl Mela; Santiago R. Balseiro; Adam Leary | 2019 | Academic literature review | Open PDF | https://hanachoi.github.io/research-papers/choi_et_al_review_display_advertising.pdf | Academic review; technical | Map of the display advertising ecosystem, auctions, targeting, advertisers, publishers, and consumers. |
| kohavi_controlled_experiments_scale | Experimentation | High | Online Controlled Experiments at Large Scale | Ron Kohavi et al. | 2013 | Academic/practitioner paper | Open PDF | https://exp-platform.com/Documents/2013%20controlledExperimentsAtScale.pdf | Written by experimentation practitioners; highly practical | A/B testing fundamentals, scale issues, organizational discipline. |
| kohavi_tang_xu_book | Experimentation | High | Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments: A Practical Guide to A/B Testing | Ron Kohavi; Diane Tang; Ya Xu | 2020 | Book | Buy/borrow; publisher previews available | https://www.cambridge.org/core/books/trustworthy-online-controlled-experiments/4EC18E75D4F1D3A576B28E0CBD49F5B9 | Practitioner-led but rigorous; book is not free in full | Best practical guide to running experiments without fooling yourself. |
| kohavi_unexpected_results | Experimentation | Useful | Unexpected Results in Online Controlled Experiments | Ron Kohavi; Roger Longbotham | 2010 | Academic/practitioner paper | Open PDF | https://kdd.org/exploration_files/v12-02-8-UR-Kohavi.pdf | Practitioner perspective; valuable failure modes | Teaches humility: experiments often produce counterintuitive results. |
| fogg_2009_behavior_model | Buyer psychology | Essential | A Behavior Model for Persuasive Design | B. J. Fogg | 2009 | Academic conference paper | Open PDF | https://www.demenzemedicinagenerale.net/images/mens-sana/Captology_Fogg_Behavior_Model.pdf | Persuasive design lens; can be misused if not ethically constrained | Motivation + ability + prompt is the simplest usable model for conversion behavior. |
| stanford_fogg_behavior_model | Buyer psychology | Useful | The Fogg Behavior Model | Stanford Behavior Design Lab | Current | Official framework page | Open web page | https://behaviordesign.stanford.edu/resources/fogg-behavior-model | Official model page; simplified | Good quick reference for the behavior model. |
| davis_tam_1989 | Software adoption | Essential | Perceived Usefulness, Perceived Ease of Use, and User Acceptance of Information Technology | Fred D. Davis | 1989 | Peer-reviewed paper | Publisher/JSTOR; summaries available | https://www.jstor.org/stable/249008 | Classic model; later models add context, norms, and habit | Core SaaS adoption equation: useful + easy to use. |
| tam_theoryhub | Software adoption | Useful | Technology Acceptance Model Overview | TheoryHub / Newcastle University | 2025 | Theory overview | Open PDF | https://open.ncl.ac.uk/theory-library/technology-acceptance-model.pdf | Secondary summary | Readable overview of TAM before/after the original Davis paper. |
| friestad_wright_1994_pkm | Skepticism and trust | Essential | The Persuasion Knowledge Model: How People Cope with Persuasion Attempts | Marian Friestad; Peter Wright | 1994 | Peer-reviewed theory paper | Publisher page; library may be needed | https://academic.oup.com/jcr/article/21/1/1/1853712 | Theory paper; requires application judgment | Explains the mental guard that activates when people notice “someone is trying to persuade me.” |
| obermiller_spangenberg_maclachlan_2005 | Skepticism and trust | High | Ad Skepticism: The Consequences of Disbelief | Carl Obermiller; Eric Spangenberg; Douglas L. MacLachlan | 2005 | Peer-reviewed paper | Publisher page/open copies | https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/00913367.2005.10639199 | Academic; specific measures of skepticism | Directly addresses your instinct to disbelieve ad claims. |
| petty_cacioppo_elm_1986 | Buyer psychology | High | The Elaboration Likelihood Model of Persuasion | Richard E. Petty; John T. Cacioppo | 1986 | Foundational persuasion theory chapter | Open author PDF | https://richardepetty.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/01/1986-advances-pettycacioppo.pdf | Classic model; not a conversion playbook by itself | Separates deep argument processing from surface-cue persuasion. |
| sahin_diffusion_2006 | Software adoption | High | Detailed Review of Rogers' Diffusion of Innovations Theory | Ismail Sahin | 2006 | Academic review paper | Open PDF | https://tilisathibodeaux.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/Sahin.pdf | Secondary review of Rogers; education-tech angle | Adoption categories, perceived attributes of innovation, uncertainty reduction. |
| christensen_jobs_2016 | Customer research | Essential | Know Your Customers' Jobs to Be Done | Clayton M. Christensen; Taddy Hall; Karen Dillon; David S. Duncan | 2016 | HBR article / strategy framework | HBR; PDF copies may exist | https://hbr.org/2016/09/know-your-customers-jobs-to-be-done | Practitioner/consulting style; use with interviews, not slogans | Teaches that customers “hire” products to make progress in a situation. |
| mckinsey_consumer_decision_journey_2009 | Customer journey | High | The Consumer Decision Journey | David Court; Dave Elzinga; Susan Mulder; Ole Jørgen Vetvik | 2009 | Consulting/practitioner model | Open web article | https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/growth-marketing-and-sales/our-insights/the-consumer-decision-journey | Consulting framework; not peer-reviewed | Useful replacement for simplistic linear funnels. |
| mckinsey_new_decision_journey_2015 | Customer journey | Useful | The New Consumer Decision Journey | McKinsey | 2015 | Consulting/practitioner model | Open web article | https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/growth-marketing-and-sales/our-insights/the-new-consumer-decision-journey | Consulting perspective | Adds digital feedback loops and active evaluation. |

Lane | 1998/2007 | Usability research paper | Open PDF | https://www.ruf.rice.edu/~lane/papers/banner_blindness.pdf | Old web context but human attention pattern remains useful | Primary evidence for why ad-like elements get ignored. |

| nng_original_eyetracking | Attention and ad avoidance | Useful | Banner Blindness: The Original Eyetracking Research | Nielsen Norman Group | 2007 | UX research article | Open web article | https://www.nngroup.com/articles/banner-blindness-original-eyetracking/ | UX consultancy; interpret alongside academic work | Useful visual attention framing for ad/landing page design. |
| nielsen_trust_ads_2021 | Skepticism and trust | High | Beyond Martech: Building Trust with Consumers and Engaging Where Sentiment Is High | Nielsen | 2021 | Industry research / trust in advertising | Open web article | https://www.nielsen.com/insights/2021/beyond-martech-building-trust-with-consumers-and-engaging-where-sentiment-is-high/ | Measurement company; still useful for channel-trust comparisons | Shows relative trust in word-of-mouth vs paid ad channels. |
| ismagilova_source_credibility_2019 | Skepticism and trust | High | The Effect of Characteristics of Source Credibility on Consumer Behaviour: A Meta-Analysis | Elvira Ismagilova et al. | 2020 | Peer-reviewed meta-analysis | Publisher page; abstracts/open versions | https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0969698918307926 | Academic meta-analysis; mostly eWOM contexts | Explains why expertise, trustworthiness, and similarity affect adoption of messages. |

| aguirre_personalization_paradox_2015 | Skepticism and trust | High | Unraveling the Personalization Paradox | Elizabeth Aguirre; Dominik Mahr; Dhruv Grewal; Ko de Ruyter; Martin Wetzels | 2015 | Peer-reviewed paper | Open institutional PDF | https://cris.maastrichtuniversity.nl/ws/files/65443620/mahr_2015_unraveling_the_personalization_paradox.pdf | Experimental settings; implications depend on current privacy norms | Shows personalization can help or backfire depending on perceived data collection. |
| ghosh_online_purchase_intention_2024 | Skepticism and trust | Useful | Meta-Analytic Review of Online Purchase Intention | Moumita Ghosh et al. | 2024 | Peer-reviewed meta-analysis | Open PDF | https://www.econstor.eu/bitstream/10419/325948/1/10.1080_23311975.2023.2296686.pdf | Broad e-commerce scope; not SaaS-specific | Good broad map of online purchase-intention drivers. |

A Large-Scale Randomized Field Experiment on Free Trial Duration in the Freemium Model | Ling Zhang; Jiang Duan | 2025 | Open-access field experiment | Open PMC/Frontiers article | https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12217587/ | One SaaS company; still valuable because randomized and large-scale | Trial length affects adoption and delayed conversion differently from immediate conversion. |

| oecd_sludge_audits_2024 | Ethics and dark patterns | Useful | Fixing Frictions: Sludge Audits Around the World | OECD | 2024 | Government/intergovernmental report | Open PDF | https://www.oecd.org/content/dam/oecd/en/publications/reports/2024/06/fixing-frictions-sludge-audits-around-the-world_1b4bbf1a/5e9bb35c-en.pdf | Public-sector lens; not commercial conversion-focused | Good practical approach to auditing harmful friction. |
